titlestmt.titleproper: Preliminary Guide to the Jules Prown Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Catherine Roach and staff of Manuscripts and Archives

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 22, 2020 at 11:30
Finding aid revision description not supplied.

did.abstract: The papers consist of correspondence, subject files and writings documenting the professional career of Jules Prown in the field of art history.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The bulk of the materials are open for research. Boxes 3 and 4 of the original accession are restricted until January 1, 2035 as established by Yale Corporation regulations. Box 5 is restricted until January 1, 2051 as established by Yale Corporation regulations. Box 7 of Accession 2019-M-0018 is restricted until January 1, 2093 as established by Yale Corporation regulations.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright is retained by the creator(s) of this collection for materials they have authored or otherwise produced. After the lifetime of the creator(s), copyright passes to Yale University. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Jules David Prown Papers (MS 1749). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Jules Prown, noted art historian and educator whose interests range from American colonial artwork to material culture, was born in Freehold, New Jersey, on March 14, 1930. He was graduated from Lafayette College in 1951 and earned masters degrees from Harvard University in Fine Arts and the University of Delaware in Early American Culture. In 1961, he obtained his doctorate degree from Harvard University, where he was the Edward R. Bacon Art Scholar. That same year, Prown went to Yale University as an instructor and later became the Paul Mellon Professor of the History of Art, a position he held until his retirement in 1999. He was involved in all aspects of art history, as a scholar, educator and curator. In addition to his academic achievements, Prown served as the curator of the Garvan and Related Collections of American Art at the Yale University Art Gallery and as director of the Yale Center for British Art from 1968 to 1976, a position he relinquished in favor of teaching and scholarship. Prown was an active member of the Yale community and served on a number of university committees and task forces.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of correspondence, subject files, and writings documenting the professional career of Jules Prown in the field of art history.

did.unittitle: American Society for Eighteenth Century Studies
Brazilian Congress of the History of Art
"Answering Questions the (American) Object Cannot Answer" session
International Congress of the History of Art [Art and National Ideas session]

title.part: American Painting from its Beginnings to the Armory Show

did.unittitle: "The Art Historian and the Computer; An Analysis of Copley's Patronage, 1753-1774"
"Benjamin West and the Use of Antiquity"
"Benjamin West's Family Picture: A Nativity in Hammersmith"
"Copley, John Singleton" [ Dictionary of American Art entry]
"The Expedition Against the Ohio Indians in 1764 under Colonel Bouquet: Two Early Drawings by Benjamin West"
"A Note on Copley's Forge of Vulcan "
"The Promise and the Perils of Context"
"Raphaelle Peale, Fruits in A Silver Basket , 1814"
"The Work of Art and Historical Scholarship"
Untitled [article about Thomas Sully manuscript notebooks]

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Materials in boxes 3 and 4 are restricted until January 1, 2035 as established by Yale Corporation regulations.

Committee on Restructuring the Faculty of Arts and Sciences

accessrestrict.p: Materials in box 5 are restricted until January 1, 2051 as established by Yale Corporation regulations.

did.unittitle: Fourth International Congress on the Enlightenment
John Singleton Copley [research notes, background materials]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The accession consists of correspondence and subject files pertaining to Jules David Prown, his work, his colleagues, former students, and teachings at Yale University. The accession also contains graduate and doctoral student correspondence and grades.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The materials are arranged into two groupings: Subject and correspondence files, and doctoral student and university files.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The bulk of the accession is open for research. Student records are restricted until 2093 as established by Yale Corporation regulations http://web.library.yale.edu/mssa/collections/research-use-of-yale-university-archives.

Yale University correspondence and general material

accessrestrict.p: Student records are restricted until 2093 as established by Yale Corporation regulations http://web.library.yale.edu/mssa/collections/research-use-of-yale-university-archives.
